content:
Thank you for the thorough review. We appreciate the recognition of our empirical methodology and the clear identification of areas for improvement.

Regarding the concern about architectural diversity: in our follow-up work, we have extended the scaling analysis to cover state-space models and mixture-of-experts architectures. The core power-law relationship holds, though the exponents differ in ways that correlate with the effective parameter utilization of each architecture.

On the topic of training data composition, we agree this is a significant limitation of the original study. We have since conducted ablation experiments across three distinct corpus compositions (web crawl, curated academic text, and code) and found that data quality shifts the scaling curves vertically but does not change their slope. These results are included in Appendix C of the revised version.
